title: EOI Points Calculator for Skilled Migration to Australia
subtitle: Visa subclasses 189 · 190 · 491 — compare multiple skills assessments side by side
sharedNote: These belong to you as the applicant and apply to every assessment: age, English, qualification, partner status, plus STEM, Australian study, regional study and NAATI bonuses.
jobsNote: An applicant may hold several skills assessments. Work experience and the Professional Year must relate to the nominated occupation, so they are entered per occupation.

boxes.stem: STEM / IT Masters by research or PhD at an Australian institution (2 yrs)

partnerStatus.partnerSkills: Partner with skills assessment + competent English

pyLabel: Professional Year (Accounting / Eng / IT, related to this occupation)
noBestPath: Select a nominated occupation to compute visa pathways
pathNote: 189 requires an MLTSSL occupation. 190 requires state nomination (+5 pts) and accepts MLTSSL / STSOL. 491 requires state or family regional nomination (+15 pts) and accepts all lists including ROL. Every pathway needs 65+ points to lodge an EOI. Note: each state also keeps its own 190/491 occupation list — the tags on the right show which states can nominate your chosen occupation. See "State list differences" under Reference.
statesIntro: Federal lists (MLTSSL / STSOL / ROL) are only the entry ticket: each state and territory keeps its own occupation list, selection method and extra requirements (residence, local work, higher English or points), adjusted through the year. 20,350 nomination places nationally in 2025–26. Once you pick an occupation, the states that can nominate it are tagged below.
statesNote: A 2025–26 snapshot — always check each state's official page.

NSW.tip: NSW work valued; ICT, health, engineering, construction first
NSW.statusNote: 2025–26 allocation fully committed; 2026-27 settings under review

VIC.how: No fixed list · ROI selection (Live in Melbourne)
VIC.tip: Health, teaching, STEM prioritised; local work & study count
VIC.statusNote: ROI intake stopped 30 Apr 2026 (allocation met); 2026-27 not yet open

QLD.tip: Onshore usually needs 6+ months of QLD work

WA.tip: General stream often needs a 6-month WA contract; mining, construction, health
WA.statusNote: Still running invitation rounds under the 2025–26 program

SA.how: Broadest state list (hundreds of onshore roles)
SA.tip: Per-occupation extra requirements; monthly invitation data
SA.statusNote: ROI intake closed 2 Jun 2026; 2026-27 list not yet published

TAS.tip: Usually 6–9 months of TAS work; settlement focus
TAS.statusNote: 2025–26 places fully used; ROI Gateway closed for maintenance pending 2026-27

ACT.how: ACT Nominated Migration Program Occupation List + Canberra Matrix
ACT.tip: Canberra residence & work history required; mostly local invitees
ACT.statusNote: Portal offline for the 2026-27 transition, reopening late Jul 2026

NT.how: Offshore MOL · onshore needs NT connection
NT.tip: About 1,650 places in 2025–26; onshore applicants need NT ties
NT.statusNote: Portal closed — 2025–26 allocation reached; 2026-27 pending confirmation

sug.stem: Complete a STEM research Masters or PhD
sug.professionalYear: Complete a Professional Year for this occupation
sug.ausStudy: Complete 2 years of full-time study in Australia
sug.mltssl: Add an MLTSSL occupation assessment to unlock subclass 189

roundsNote: Subclass 189 now runs a few large, roughly quarterly rounds ranked by occupation. 65 points is only the floor for high-demand trades; professional, STEM and medical occupations typically needed 85–115 points in the same round.
tlNote: The projection is generated from the months you enter next to each field above (birth, English test, work periods, assessment issue) — age-bracket drops, work milestones and credential expiries over the next 5 years. Work experience follows the 10-year window rule: an empty end month means the job is ongoing.
tlNaatiHint: Enabled with the NAATI bonus above
tlEndBeforeStart: End month is before the start
tlFutureBirth: Birth month must be in the past
tlOver45: Age 45+ — no longer eligible for these visas
tlEmpty: Enter a month next to any field above (birth month, work start month…) to draw the projection.

tl.ausWorkDrop: Australian work in the 10-yr window falls below the {{years}}-year mark
tl.overseasWorkDrop: Overseas work in the 10-yr window falls below the {{years}}-year mark

tlChartSummary: Projected score timeline from {{from}} to {{to}}, starting at {{score}} points with {{n}} events.
pr191Note: Subclass 491/494 is provisional — after {{years}} years of regional-compliant residence, holders can apply for the permanent Subclass 191. Enter your 491/494 grant month to project your earliest eligibility date.
pr191Empty: Enter a grant month to see your projected eligibility date.
pr191EligibleNow: You've held the visa long enough — eligible now, subject to the conditions below.
pr191ReqRegional: Compliance with visa condition 8579 throughout: live, work and study only in a designated regional area (excludes Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Gold Coast and Perth).
pr191ReqIncome: {{n}} lodged ATO Notices of Assessment within the visa's {{validity}}-year validity — any reported income counts, there is no fixed minimum-income figure.
pr191ReqHealth: Standard health and character (police clearance) requirements at time of application.
pr191Fee: Application charge: {{primary}} primary applicant · +{{partner}} partner (18+) · +{{child}} child, from 1 Jul 2026.
pr191Disclaimer: A guide only — this cannot verify your actual regional residence or income-year compliance. Confirm current requirements with the Department of Home Affairs before applying.
feesNote: A rough order-of-magnitude budget from the criteria and occupations you've entered above — not a quote. Each occupation you've entered gets its own skills-assessment fee (you'd need a separate assessment for each one you keep as an option); the visa application and state nomination fees apply once, for whichever pathway currently scores highest. Real costs vary by assessing body, agent fees (if any) and exchange rate.
feesEmpty: Select an occupation above to see an estimate.
feesAssessmentRange: typical range, not this occupation's exact fee
feesNominationFree: free in at least one eligible state
feesDisclaimer: Figures are AUD estimates compiled 2026-07 from public fee schedules and may already be out of date — confirm current charges with the Department of Home Affairs, your assessing authority and the nominating state before budgeting.
spNote: 189/190/491 are points-tested; 482 and 186 are not — an employer sponsors you directly against a checklist (occupation gate, salary threshold, experience, age, English). This page checks the occupations, English level and age already entered on the Independent Migration page against all four streams — a different segment of the migration system worth knowing about if you can't yet reach 65 points.
spPageSubtitle: Visa subclasses 482 · 186 — a separate, non-points-tested pathway
spHasSponsor: I have (or a prospective employer has offered) sponsorship
spTrtEligible: I already hold a 482/457 visa with ≥2 years in this occupation for the nominating employer
spTrtHint: Temporary Residence Transition (TRT) pathway to subclass 186 — reduced from 3 to 2 years in Nov 2023; the occupation-list requirement was removed the same year.
spAgeUnknown: Age unknown — enter a birth month on the Independent Migration page to check the 186 age limit
spEmpty: No occupation entered yet — nothing to check yet.
spGateCsol: Occupation not on the Core Skills Occupation List (CSOL)
spGateSalaryCsit: Salary must reach the Core Skills Income Threshold
spGateSalarySsit: Salary must reach the Specialist Skills Income Threshold
spGateSpecialistGroup: Occupation is a Technician/Trades, Machinery Operator or Labourer role (excluded from Specialist Skills)
spGateExperience: Needs {{years}}+ years of relevant work experience
spGateEnglish: Needs a recorded English level (Independent Migration page)
spGateTrtEligible: Needs ≥2 years on a 482/457 with the nominating employer in this occupation
spDisclaimer: A screening checklist only, not a nomination assessment — reuses the occupations, English level and age entered on the Independent Migration page (so CSOL-only occupations outside that list aren't checkable here), and approximates work experience from the Australian/overseas work brackets entered there rather than counting employment in the last 5 years exactly as the visa criteria define it. CSIT/SSIT and the 456-occupation CSOL are current as of 2026-07; some CSOL occupations carry extra salary or duty caveats not modelled here. Confirm eligibility with your employer and a registered migration agent before proceeding.
disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Immigration rules change frequently — always verify with the Department of Home Affairs or a registered migration agent.
cardFoot: Reference only · verify with the Department of Home Affairs
pwaIosHintPre: Install this calculator as an app: tap the Share button
pwaIosHintPost: in Safari, then choose "Add to Home Screen".
pwaChromiumHint: Install this calculator as an app — it opens in its own window and works offline.
